key_points: Biodiversity means the variety of plants, animals, and microorganisms
Forests support wildlife and maintain ecological balance
Conservation means protecting forests and wildlife
The Wildlife Protection Act was implemented in 1972
Project Tiger was launched in 1973 to protect tigers
examples: Kaziranga National Park protects the one-horned rhinoceros
Gir National Park protects Asiatic lions
Sacred groves are forest patches protected due to religious beliefs
Chipko Movement involved villagers hugging trees
Corbett National Park is a tiger reserve
formulas: Biodiversity = Variety of living organisms in an ecosystem
Conservation = Protection and sustainable use of natural resources
Forest conservation = Protection of forests and wildlife habitats
exam_tips: Remember important years such as 1972 for Wildlife Protection Act
Learn examples of national parks and wildlife sanctuaries
Understand the meaning of biodiversity and conservation
Use examples to explain conservation methods
